title: Mildew on Linen
timeEstimate: 30–90 minutes home treatment; structural mildew or vintage linen = pro / remediation specialist
stainChemistry: Mildew on linen is a living fungal colony — fungal roots penetrate fiber, spores release on contact, metabolic byproducts cause gray / black / green staining, and microbial volatile organic compounds cause the musty odor. Linen is cellulose (similar to cotton) and tolerates oxygen-bleach chemistry, but it's more delicate than cotton when wet — linen wrinkles aggressively and structured / lined / vintage linen warps under aggressive handling. Two cases: (1) SURFACE MILDEW on simple flat washable linen — tractable home job. (2) STRUCTURAL MILDEW — heavily saturated linen, vintage / antique pieces with fiber-level penetration, or linen attached to a structure (curtains in a damp basement, lined drapery with mold inside the lining) — that's a remediation / specialty conservator situation, not a stain. The hard line that separates the two is moisture: if the linen is in a permanently damp environment, every cleaning is temporary until the moisture source is fixed. The second hard line is chlorine bleach — strips dye on dyed linen, weakens cellulose at concentration, and doesn't fix the cause.
whyThisWorks: OxiClean / sodium percarbonate cool soak on white or color-fast washable linen kills surface mildew organisms and oxidizes pigmented byproducts. Linen tolerates this without the damage chlorine bleach causes. 3% peroxide on whites adds a residual-stain pass. Sun-dry adds UV antimicrobial finish AND drives moisture out. The hard prerequisite: moisture-source fix. Mildew returns to the same spot if humidity, leak, or condensation remain. Without that fix, every cleaning is temporary.
defaultAssumption: Simple flat washable linen — napkin, plain shirt, basic tablecloth, plain pillowcase — with surface mildew from a damp closet, basement storage, or extended damp folding. Structured / lined / vintage / dyed-saturated linen, structural mildew, or curtains attached to wet windows = pro or remediation specialist.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Care label, good light, N95 mask, gloves
spottingProtocol.instruction: Dry-clean-only / structured / lined / vintage linen = pro from minute one. Simple flat washable linen = home candidate. PPE: N95 mask + gloves before touching mildew. Inspect: visible spots vs heavy saturation through the fiber. Heavy saturation, fiber weakness, or musty smell from a lining = remediation.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Soft brush, HEPA vacuum, outdoor space
spottingProtocol.instruction: Outdoors. Soft-brush spores away from face, downwind. HEPA vacuum follows the brush. Never indoors — spores aerosolize and recolonize new fabric.

spottingProtocol.technique: Oxygen bleach kills organism + oxidizes pigment
spottingProtocol.equipment: OxiClean per label, COOL water, basin
spottingProtocol.instruction: Mix OxiClean per label in COOL water. Submerge the linen 30 min – 2 hr. Check at 30 min — pull when pigment clears. Cool rinse, then second cool wash with normal detergent.

spottingProtocol.equipment: 3% H₂O₂ on damp cloth or as soak additive
spottingProtocol.instruction: WHITE or very light linen only. Hidden-area test on dyed linen first. 3% peroxide damp cloth or as add to OxiClean soak. Cool rinse.

spottingProtocol.dwellTime: Half-day minimum, multi-day for thick weave
spottingProtocol.instruction: Hang in direct sunlight several hours. UV kills residual spores and drives moisture out. AIR-DRY only — NEVER hot dryer until fully clear (heat sets pigmented byproducts).

Identify and fix the moisture source — humidity above 60%, basement leak, condensation, damp closet. Without this, mildew returns to the same fabric. Dehumidifier under 50% RH, repair leaks, add ventilation. Don't store linen in a damp space.

steps: 1. Triage — surface vs structural; structured / vintage = pro.
2. PPE up. Outdoor HEPA vacuum and brush spore removal.
4. 3% H₂O₂ for residual pigment on white / light linen, hidden test.
5. Sun-dry plus AIR-DRY. NO heat until clear.
6. Moisture-source fix — dehumidifier, leak repair, ventilation.
professionalProtocol.temperature: cool throughout — heat sets pigmented byproducts
warnings: Structured / lined / vintage linen = pro from minute one
Structural mildew (heavy fiber penetration, lining contamination, fabric weakness) = remediation specialist
NEVER chlorine bleach — strips color, weakens cellulose at concentration
PPE required — mildew spores are inhalation hazards
Hidden test on dyed linen before peroxide
Moisture-source fix is non-negotiable for durability

homeSolutions.agent: Mildew Is a Living Organism, Not a Stain
homeSolutions.instruction: Mildew is fungus growing on your linen. Surface mildew on a washable piece is fixable. Heavy saturation, vintage linen, fiber weakness, or mold inside a lining is a remediation / conservator job, not a peroxide bottle. Don't breathe spores. N95 mask and gloves before you start. Open windows.

Dry-clean-only linen, structured pieces (lined jackets, drapery), or vintage linen all narrow your home options sharply — that's pro from minute one. Simple flat washable linen (napkin, plain shirt, basic tablecloth, pillowcase) = home candidate. If the linen feels brittle or the fabric is weakening, that's fiber-level damage from prolonged mildew — pro.

homeSolutions.agent: Take It Outside and Get the Loose Spores Off
homeSolutions.instruction: Outdoors only. Soft-brush the visible spores away from your face, downwind. Follow with a HEPA vacuum. Never do this indoors — spores aerosolize and start growing on other fabric in the room. Wear the N95 the whole time.

homeSolutions.agent: OxiClean Cool Soak — 30 Minutes to 2 Hours
homeSolutions.instruction: Mix OxiClean per label in COOL water. Submerge the linen. Check every 30 minutes — pull when the pigment clears. Cool rinse, then a second cool wash with normal detergent. NEVER hot water — heat sets pigmented mildew byproducts into linen.

homeSolutions.agent: 3% Peroxide for Residual Pigment on White Linen
homeSolutions.instruction: WHITE or very light linen only: 3% drugstore peroxide on a damp cloth, or add to the OxiClean soak. Hidden-area test on dyed linen first — peroxide can lighten color. Skip on dyed unless hidden test passes.

Hang the linen in direct sunlight for several hours. UV kills residual spores and drives moisture out of the weave. AIR-DRY only — NEVER hot dryer until you've confirmed the pigment is fully gone. Heat sets residual mildew byproducts.

homeSolutions.agent: Fix the Moisture Source — This Is Not Optional
homeSolutions.instruction: Mildew returns to the same fabric if the conditions return. Identify the cause: damp closet, basement humidity, condensation, leak, storage in a wet space. Run a dehumidifier under 50% relative humidity. Repair leaks. Don't fold and store linen damp. Without this, every cleaning is temporary.

steps: 2. Care label triage — dry-clean-only / structured / vintage = pro.
3. Outdoor brush + HEPA vacuum spore removal away from face.
5. 3% peroxide for residual pigment on white linen only, hidden test on dyed.
6. Sun-dry several hours + AIR-DRY. NEVER hot dryer until clear.
7. Fix the moisture source — dehumidifier, leak repair, dry storage.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if linen is dry-clean-only, structured, lined, vintage, or showing fiber weakness. Stop if mildew returns within days — moisture source needs professional diagnosis. Stop after 2 OxiClean soaks plus sun-dry without progress.
warnings: Structured / lined / vintage linen = pro from minute one
Heavy saturation or fiber weakness = remediation, not stain
NEVER chlorine bleach — strips color, weakens cellulose
NEVER hot dryer until pigment fully cleared
PPE required — mildew spores are inhalation hazards

neverDo: Never treat structured / lined / vintage linen at home
Never chlorine bleach on linen mildew
Never brush mildew indoors — spores aerosolize
Never skip PPE — N95 and gloves required
Never hot water or hot dryer until pigment cleared
fiberSensitivities: White / light washable linen: tolerates OxiClean cool soak + 3% peroxide
Dyed linen: hidden test mandatory before peroxide
Structured / lined linen: pro — warps under wet handling
Vintage / antique linen: pro — fiber fragility under oxidizers
Heavily saturated linen with fiber weakness: remediation, not cleaning

materialWarnings: Structured / vintage linen = pro from minute one
Heavy saturation or fiber weakness = remediation specialist territory
No chlorine bleach — strips dye and weakens cellulose
Moisture-source fix is part of the protocol, not optional
PPE is required — spore inhalation is a real hazard

escalation.when: Dry-clean-only / structured / lined / vintage linen. Heavy saturation through the weave or fiber weakness from prolonged mildew. Mildew returns within days of home cleaning (moisture source not fixed). 2 home OxiClean soaks plus sun-dry plateau. Lining contamination on a structured piece. Health concerns — anyone with asthma, immune issues, or mold sensitivity in the home.
escalation.whatToTell: "Mildew on [white / light / dyed / vintage / structured] linen, [surface only / heavy saturation / fiber weakness], age [days / weeks / months], moisture source [identified — humidity / leak / unknown], home-treated with [PPE + outdoor HEPA + OxiClean cool soak + 3% peroxide on white + sun-dry] — no chlorine bleach, no hot. [Recurring within days / not improving / suspect lining contamination / suspect fiber damage]."
escalation.specialistType: professional dry cleaner with mildew / mold experience for surface mildew on simple linen; specialty textile conservator for vintage or heavily saturated pieces; licensed mold remediation specialist if structural / lining contamination or recurring environmental source

contextQuestions: Simple flat washable linen, structured / lined, or vintage?
Surface visible spots, or has the mildew penetrated the weave?
Where is the moisture coming from — humidity, damp closet, leak, condensation?
How long has the linen been stored damp?
Anyone in the home with asthma or mold sensitivity?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Care label is the first gate — washable simple linen proceeds, structured / lined / vintage goes to pro. Heavy fiber-level saturation means the linen has structural damage and a stain protocol won't recover it. Moisture-source fix is what makes the cleaning durable. Health-sensitive household = pro / remediation regardless of fabric. Linen wrinkles aggressively wet — handle gently, no aggressive agitation.
